title: rhel9 + log-driver=journald : missing first line
body:
On RHEL9:
```console
# podman run --log-driver=journald  --name foo quay.io/libpod/testimage:20210610  sh -c 'echo a;echo b'
a
b
# podman logs foo
b
```
Seems to be 100% reproducible, and is the common element behind a large number of the RHEL9 gating tests.

podman-3.3.0-0.18.module+el9beta+12201+6a03aeff.x86_64

comments.body:
@edsantiago 
journalctl -b | grep conmon
Does this show something like:

Aug 17 16:06:03 localhost.localdomain conmon[99837]: a
Aug 17 16:06:03 localhost.localdomain conmon[99837]: b

Or does it just show `: a`?

Oh, good idea.
```console
# podman run --log-driver=journald --name c1 quay.io/libpod/testimage:20210610 sh -c 'date;echo aaa1;sleep 10;echo aaa2;date'
Tue Aug 17 20:13:32 UTC 2021
aaa1
aaa2
Tue Aug 17 20:13:42 UTC 2021
# podman logs c1
aaa1
aaa2
Tue Aug 17 20:13:42 UTC 2021
# journalctl -b | grep -3 aaa
Aug 17 16:13:32 ci-vm-10-0-138-74.hosted.upshift.rdu2.redhat.com systemd[1]: Started libpod-conmon-883ba75eedd5cfa8ce09509a9288419f1af46cdae24687ef715fa5d19e03d64c.scope.
Aug 17 16:13:32 ci-vm-10-0-138-74.hosted.upshift.rdu2.redhat.com systemd[1]: Started libcrun container.
Aug 17 16:13:32 ci-vm-10-0-138-74.hosted.upshift.rdu2.redhat.com conmon[21985]: Tue Aug 17 20:13:32 UTC 2021
Aug 17 16:13:32 ci-vm-10-0-138-74.hosted.upshift.rdu2.redhat.com conmon[21985]: aaa1
Aug 17 16:13:42 ci-vm-10-0-138-74.hosted.upshift.rdu2.redhat.com conmon[21985]: aaa2
Aug 17 16:13:42 ci-vm-10-0-138-74.hosted.upshift.rdu2.redhat.com conmon[21985]: Tue Aug 17 20:13:42 UTC 2021
Aug 17 16:13:42 ci-vm-10-0-138-74.hosted.upshift.rdu2.redhat.com systemd[1]: libpod-883ba75eedd5cfa8ce09509a9288419f1af46cdae24687ef715fa5d19e03d64c.scope: Deactivated successfully.
Aug 17 16:13:42 ci-vm-10-0-138-74.hosted.upshift.rdu2.redhat.com audit[22032]: NETFILTER_CFG table=filter:430 family=2 entries=1 op=nft_unregister_rule pid=22032 subj=unconfined_u:system_r:iptables_t:s0 comm="iptables"
```
So reading the logs is dropping the first message.  Writing looks correct.
May be related to Podman setting the cursor to the wrong position.
